The original water source was from Lake Huron, which for many years did not dissolve the lead into the water so there was no problem. When they switched to using raw water from the Flint River, the water chemistry (ph I think) caused the lead compounds to dissolve into the water.

When Flint switched their water source from Lake Huron to the Flint River, they needed to raise the pH of the water so it didn't leach heavy metals (including lead) from the pipes. The way to do this is to add sodium hydroxide, which they didn't do but are doing now.

It's the whole GOP.
Remember in '14 Gov.Snyder(R-MI) put an emergency manager over Flint? W/broad, unchecked powers, he switched water from Lake Huron to Flint River, exposing residents to pipes w/high lead levels.
They don't care about poisoning people; they only care about getting caught.

Who needs car batteries to be poisoned by lead; in April 2014, Flint Michigan switched its water source from Lake Huron to the Flint River to save money. The city failed to add corrosion control treatment to the river water and caused lead to leach from pipes into the drinking water. To save $ !!!

Lead pipes for water actually are fine as long as you have corrosion control measures. They didn't do that when they switched from Lake Huron to the Flint River water. Really interesting how it all works too. Did lots of research on it. Still good to replace in case of accidents now tho
